verses.text: It displeases Jonah; he says he fled because he knew God was gracious, and asks to die. Grudging heart. He resents the very mercy that makes God worthy of worship.
It displeases Jonah; he says he fled because he knew God was gracious, and asks to die. Grudging heart. He resents the very mercy that makes God worthy of worship.
It displeases Jonah; he says he fled because he knew God was gracious, and asks to die. Grudging heart. He resents the very mercy that makes God worthy of worship.
God asks, 'Doest thou well to be angry?' Jonah makes a booth and waits to see the city's fate. Sullen watching. He hopes still for judgment rather than mercy.
God asks, 'Doest thou well to be angry?' Jonah makes a booth and waits to see the city's fate. Sullen watching. He hopes still for judgment rather than mercy.
God prepares a gourd for shade, then a worm to wither it; the sun beats down, and Jonah faints and longs to die. A small loss. His comfort over a plant exposes his hard heart toward men.
God prepares a gourd for shade, then a worm to wither it; the sun beats down, and Jonah faints and longs to die. A small loss. His comfort over a plant exposes his hard heart toward men.
God prepares a gourd for shade, then a worm to wither it; the sun beats down, and Jonah faints and longs to die. A small loss. His comfort over a plant exposes his hard heart toward men.

Art thou angry for the gourd, on which thou hadst pity? 'And should not I spare Nineveh,' with its thousands who know not their right hand? God's great pity. The book ends with mercy's question, leaving us to answer.
